prompt:
A single, powerful, label‑free image that makes the viewer feel grief and uneasy watchfulness about the last observed COVID‑19 cases around the world. 

Visual metaphor: a dark, quiet global landscape at twilight where each country is represented by a small, dim figure standing on its geographic location; most figures are almost invisible or faded to gray, but a few scattered figures in a handful of countries are still faintly glowing with a sickly pale blue light, symbolizing the last places where cases were still observed. The glowing figures are few and isolated, surrounded by vast darkness, emphasizing that the pandemic is not fully over but is now concentrated in a small number of places.

Composition: wide, slightly elevated view of the world map as a gentle 3D terrain; the glowing figures are in the foreground and midground, clearly visible but small against the vast dark continents; no text, no numbers, no axes, no legends, no bars, no lines, no pie slices, no standard chart elements.

Color palette: deep indigo and charcoal for the empty continents, muted gray for faded figures, and a cold, unnatural pale blue‑white glow for the few remaining “case” figures; a subtle, almost imperceptible warm amber glow on the horizon to hint at fragile hope.

Style: cinematic, photorealistic with a slightly painterly touch, high detail, dramatic low‑key lighting, somber and contemplative mood, magazine‑cover quality.

Strict constraints: no text of any kind, no labels, no legends, no axes, no standard chart types (bar, line, pie, scatter) even stylized, single frame only, minimum 1024px on the short side.
insight: As of the last reporting date in the OWID hospitalization dataset, only a handful of countries still recorded active COVID‑19 hospitalizations while the vast majority of the world had gone months with zero observed cases, showing the pandemic’s final concentration in a few isolated places.
